create an Inventory Committee who shall be responsible for the actual count to ascertain the existence, completeness and condition of all PPEs owned by the agency and prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and follow the specific guidelines and procedures as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 accordingly;
prepare the PPELCs and consistently update the RPCPPEs and revise the same using the prescribed formats; and
reconcile the current balances of PPE accounts in the Statement of Financial Position as against the general ledger of PPE, the PPE ledger cards against the general ledger and the PPELCs against the RPCPPE to support the balances of PPE in the Statement of Financial Position.

allocate budget for the registration of the Land recognized in the financial statements; and
initiate the processes in securing the transfer certificates of title (TCT) on the purchased lots to support claims of ownership to fully protect the interest of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management direct the:
Municipal Accountant to dig from previous years’ records the details and information needed to ascertain the correctness and completeness of the beginning balances of all the LGUs infrastructure assets;
Municipal Accountant to prepare the subsidiary ledgers, lapsing schedules, complete PPELC and disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements to support the balance of the Road Network System (1-07-03-010) account; and
General Services Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in conducting the inventory of all local roads and comply with the records and reports required under COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that Management direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
compute and record annual depreciation on depreciable properties to properly state the amount of PPE and net income in the Financial Statements;
regularly prepare a lapsing schedule of PPE to serve as guide in the computation of annual depreciation; and
prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
cause for the immediate remittance of the past due taxes withheld;
maintain and consistently update the subsidiary ledger for Due to BIR (202-01-020) account to properly reflect the balance for monthly remittance; and
facilitate the monthly remittance in full and within the prescribed period of all taxes to be withheld to avoid possible penalties.

exercise prudence in the utilization of fund for transportation of LGU personnel by maximizing the Municipality’s available motor vehicles in the conduct of barangay visitations.

We recommended that Management:
require the different offices/end-users in the agency to prepare and submit their Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) incorporating therein all their procurement requirements for the year and have them duly evaluated and reviewed for consolidation into a comprehensive, workable and realistic APP which would reflect the details of the procurement activities that they seek to undertake within the year;
strictly follow the procurement methods and activities as stated in the APP; and
whenever practicable, consider consolidation of purchase requests for commonly-used supplies needed by the different offices and adopt bulk purchases to promote efficiency in the procurement process.

observations.recommendation: Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to immediately submit the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment as of December 31, 2014 to the COA Auditor, and thereafter cause for the submission of the same report not later than 31st day of January of each year as required under Section 66, Volume II of the Manual on NGAS for Local Government Units.
observations.managementAction: The Management conducted their annual inventory of all their properties and the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment was submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: Submit immediately all the required supporting documents; avoid facilitating the processing of the claims without complete documentations to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Claims were still paid despite the inadequacy of the supporting documents.
observations.reason: Non-familiarization/ the need to revisit the revised guidelines and documentary requirements for common government transactions prescribed under COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14,2012.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant transfers all unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to the Trust Liability-DRRM account to help ensure that the fund would be used solely on activities supporting disaster risk reduction and management. For accurate reporting and disclosure, we recommended that the concerned official includes in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF and to reconcile the discrepancy between the LDRRMF Utilization Report and breakdown of utilization submitted.
observations.managementAction: Transfer to trust liability account up to 2015 unexpended balance only.
observations.reason: Non transfer to the trust liability account of CY 2016 unexpended balance of the 30% LDRRM Fund.

observations.recommendation: Management set aside the yearly mandatory deduction of not less than 5% for GAD fund based on the total annual budget appropriations,
observations.managementAction: Management set aside the yearly mandatory deduction of not less than 5% for GAD fund based on the total annual budget appropriations.

observations.recommendation: Management appoints the required GAD Focal point, who shall devote sufficient time for GAD mainstreaming, identifying gender issues, planning of its activities and monitoring its implementing results thus properly addressing the Municipality’s gender-related issues.
observations.managementAction: Appointment of GAD Focal Point.
observations.reason: Only a GAD Officer in charge has been appointed.
